ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io sm11-94. National Pokedex no. 680 matches. Pair of face-marked swords linked by chain is the canonical Doublade design — no other Pokemon shares this specific form.

trivia_bullets.text: Doublade appears in Sun & Moon — Unified Minds (2019) as no. 94/236, an Uncommon. National Pokedex no. 680, Ghost/Steel-type, introduced in Generation VI (Kalos).

The Honedge line (Honedge → Doublade → Aegislash) depicts progressive spirit-possession of a sword. Honedge is a single sword; Doublade is two swords coordinated telepathically after the spirit divides; Aegislash adds a shield and switches between Blade Forme (offensive) and Shield Forme (defensive) mid-battle.
"When Honedge evolves, it divides into two swords, which cooperate via telepathy to coordinate attacks and slash their enemies to ribbons."
Aegislash (Doublade's final evolution) was Smogon Uber-banned in Gen VI-VII due to base 150 offensive/defensive stat cycling with King's Shield. Nerfed in Gen VIII (150→140) and returned to OU tier.
sources: WebSearch: Sportskeeda 'Why was Aegislash considered an Uber Pokemon'

related_cards.name: Aegislash
related_cards.note: Final evolution, former Uber-tier competitive threat
